contentBlocks.original:
जय पद्मपलाशाक्षि जय त्वं श्रीपतिप्रिये ।
जय मातर्महालक्ष्मि संसारार्णवतारिणि ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jaya padmapalāśākṣi jaya tvaṃ śrīpatipriyē ।
jaya mātarmahālakṣmi saṃsārārṇavatāriṇi ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 1:] Victory to you, O lotus-petal-eyed! Victory to you, O beloved of the Lord of Śrī (Viṣṇu)! Victory, O Mother Mahālakṣmī, O ferryman across the ocean of worldly existence!
contentBlocks.meanings: jaya = victory | padma-palāśa-akṣi = O lotus-petal-eyed | śrī-pati-priyē = O beloved of the Lord of Śrī (Viṣṇu) | mātar = O Mother | mahālakṣmi = O Mahālakṣmī | saṃsāra-arṇava-tāriṇi = O ferryman across the ocean of worldly existence

contentBlocks.original:
महालक्ष्मि नमस्तुभ्यं नमस्तुभ्यं सुरेश्वरि ।
हरिप्रिये नमस्तुभ्यं नमस्तुभ्यं दयानिधे ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahālakṣmi namastubhyaṃ namastubhyaṃ surēśvari ।
haripriyē namastubhyaṃ namastubhyaṃ dayānidhē ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 2:] O Mahālakṣmī, salutation to you, salutation to you, O Queen of the gods! O beloved of Hari, salutation to you, salutation to you, O treasure-house of compassion!
contentBlocks.meanings: mahālakṣmi = O Mahālakṣmī | namastubhyaṃ = salutation to you | surēśvari = O Queen of the gods | haripriyē = O beloved of Hari | dayānidhē = O treasure-house of compassion

contentBlocks.original:
पद्मालये नमस्तुभ्यं नमस्तुभ्यं च सर्वदे ।
सर्वभूतहितार्थाय वसुवृष्टिं सदा कुरु ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
padmālayē namastubhyaṃ namastubhyaṃ cha sarvadē ।
sarvabhūtahitārthāya vasuvṛṣṭiṃ sadā kuru ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 3:] O lotus-dweller, salutation to you, salutation to you, O bestower of all! For the welfare of all beings, always shower a rain of wealth upon [us].
contentBlocks.meanings: padmālayē = O dweller in the lotus | sarvadē = O bestower of all | sarva-bhūta-hita-arthāya = for the benefit of all beings | vasu-vṛṣṭiṃ = a rain of wealth | sadā kuru = always bring about

contentBlocks.original:
जगन्मातर्नमस्तुभ्यं नमस्तुभ्यं दयानिधे ।
दयावति नमस्तुभ्यं विश्वेश्वरि नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jaganmātarnamastubhyaṃ namastubhyaṃ dayānidhē ।
dayāvati namastubhyaṃ viśvēśvari namō'stu tē ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 4:] O Mother of the world, salutation to you, salutation to you, O treasure of compassion! O compassionate one, salutation to you! O ruler of the universe, salutation to you!
contentBlocks.meanings: jagat-mātar = O Mother of the world | dayānidhē = O treasure of compassion | dayāvati = O compassionate one | viśvēśvari = O ruler of the universe | namō'stu tē = salutation to you

contentBlocks.original:
नमः क्षीरार्णवसुते नमस्त्रैलोक्यधारिणि ।
वसुवृष्टे नमस्तुभ्यं रक्ष मां शरणागतम् ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namaḥ kṣīrārṇavasutē namastrailōkyadhāriṇi ।
vasuvṛṣṭē namastubhyaṃ rakṣa māṃ śaraṇāgatam ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 5:] Salutation to the daughter of the milk-ocean! Salutation to the sustainer of the three worlds! O showerer of wealth, salutation to you — protect me who have sought refuge in you.
contentBlocks.meanings: kṣīra-arṇava-sutē = O daughter of the milk-ocean | namaḥ = salutation | trailōkya-dhāriṇi = O sustainer of the three worlds | vasu-vṛṣṭē = O showerer of wealth | rakṣa māṃ śaraṇāgatam = protect me who have taken refuge

contentBlocks.original:
रक्ष त्वं देवदेवेशि देवदेवस्य वल्लभे ।
दारिद्र्यात्त्राहि मां लक्ष्मि कृपां कुरु ममोपरि ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rakṣa tvaṃ dēvadēvēśi dēvadēvasya vallabhē ।
dāridryāttrāhi māṃ lakṣmi kṛpāṃ kuru mamōpari ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 6:] Protect me, O Queen of the Lord of gods, O beloved of the God of gods! O Lakṣmī — save me from poverty; show your grace upon me.
contentBlocks.meanings: dēva-dēvēśi = O Queen of the lord of gods | dēva-dēvasya vallabhē = O beloved of the God of gods | dāridryāt trāhi = save from poverty | lakṣmi = O Lakṣmī | kṛpāṃ kuru mama upari = show grace upon me

contentBlocks.original:
नमस्त्रैलोक्यजननि नमस्त्रैलोक्यपावनि ।
ब्रह्मादयो नमन्ति त्वां जगदानन्ददायिनि ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namastrailōkyajanani namastrailōkyapāvani ।
brahmādayō namanti tvāṃ jagadānandadāyini ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 7:] Salutation to the mother of the three worlds! Salutation to the purifier of the three worlds! O bestower of bliss to all the world — Brahmā and the other gods bow to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: namaḥ trailōkya-janani = salutation to the mother of the three worlds | trailōkya-pāvani = purifier of the three worlds | brahma-ādayaḥ = Brahmā and others | namanti tvāṃ = bow to you | jagad-ānanda-dāyini = O bestower of bliss to all the world

contentBlocks.original:
विष्णुप्रिये नमस्तुभ्यं नमस्तुभ्यं जगद्धिते ।
आर्तिहन्त्रि नमस्तुभ्यं समृद्धिं कुरु मे सदा ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viṣṇupriyē namastubhyaṃ namastubhyaṃ jagaddhitē ।
ārtihantri namastubhyaṃ samṛddhiṃ kuru mē sadā ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 8:] O beloved of Viṣṇu, salutation to you, salutation to you, O benefactress of the world! O destroyer of suffering, salutation to you — always bring me prosperity.
contentBlocks.meanings: viṣṇu-priyē = O beloved of Viṣṇu | jagad-hitē = O benefactress of the world | ārti-hantrī = O destroyer of suffering | samṛddhiṃ kuru mē sadā = always bring me prosperity/abundance

contentBlocks.original:
अब्जवासे नमस्तुभ्यं चपलायै नमो नमः ।
चञ्चलायै नमस्तुभ्यं ललितायै नमो नमः ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
abjavāsē namastubhyaṃ chapalāyai namō namaḥ ।
chañchalāyai namastubhyaṃ lalitāyai namō namaḥ ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 9:] O lotus-dweller, salutation to you! Salutation, salutation to the nimble one! Salutation to the playful, restless one! Salutation, salutation to the graceful one!
contentBlocks.meanings: abja-vāsē = O lotus-dweller | chapalāyai = to the nimble/quick-moving one | chañchalāyai = to the restless/playful one | lalitāyai = to the graceful/gracious one | namō namaḥ = salutation, salutation

contentBlocks.original:
नमः प्रद्युम्नजननि मातस्तुभ्यं नमो नमः ।
परिपालय मां मातः मां तुभ्यं शरणागतम् ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namaḥ pradyumnajanani mātastubhyaṃ namō namaḥ ।
paripālaya māṃ mātaḥ māṃ tubhyaṃ śaraṇāgatam ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 10:] Salutation to the mother of Pradyumna! O Mother, salutation, salutation to you! O Mother, protect me who have taken refuge in you.
contentBlocks.meanings: namaḥ pradyumna-janani = salutation to the mother of Pradyumna (Kāmadēva reborn as Kṛṣṇa's son) | mātar = O Mother | namō namaḥ = salutation, salutation | paripālaya māṃ = protect me fully | śaraṇāgatam = who have taken refuge

contentBlocks.original:
शरण्ये त्वां प्रपन्नोऽस्मि कमले कमलालये ।
त्राहि त्राहि महालक्ष्मि परित्राणपरायणे ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śaraṇyē tvāṃ prapannō'smi kamalē kamalālayē ।
trāhi trāhi mahālakṣmi paritrāṇaparāyaṇē ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 11:] O refuge-giver, O Kamalā, O lotus-dweller — I have surrendered to you. Save me! Save me! O Mahālakṣmī, O you who are dedicated to protecting [devotees]!
contentBlocks.meanings: śaraṇyē = O refuge-giver | tvāṃ prapannaḥ asmi = I have surrendered to you | kamalē = O Kamalā | kamalā-layē = O dweller in the lotus | trāhi trāhi = save me, save me | paritrāṇa-parāyaṇē = O one devoted to [giving] protection

contentBlocks.original:
पाण्डित्यं शोभते नैव न शोभन्ते गुणा नरे ।
शीलत्वं नैव शोभेत महालक्ष्मि त्वया विना ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pāṇḍityaṃ śōbhatē naiva na śōbhantē guṇā narē ।
śīlatvaṃ naiva śōbhēta mahālakṣmi tvayā vinā ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 12:] Without you, O Mahālakṣmī — learning does not shine; virtues do not shine in a person; good character does not shine.
contentBlocks.meanings: pāṇḍityaṃ = learning, scholarship | śōbhatē naiva = does not shine at all | guṇā = virtues | narē = in a person | śīlatvaṃ = good character | naiva śōbhēta = does not shine | mahālakṣmi tvayā vinā = without you, O Mahālakṣmī

contentBlocks.original:
तावद्विराजते रूपं तावच्छीलं विराजते ।
तावद्गुणा नराणां च यावल्लक्ष्मीः प्रसीदति ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tāvadvirājatē rūpaṃ tāvachChīlaṃ virājatē ।
tāvadguṇā narāṇāṃ cha yāvallakṣmīḥ prasīdati ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 13:] Only as long as Lakṣmī is gracious does beauty shine; only as long does character shine; only as long do the virtues of people shine.
contentBlocks.meanings: tāvad = only as long [as] | virājatē = shines, is resplendent | rūpaṃ = beauty | śīlaṃ = character | guṇā narāṇāṃ = virtues of people | yāvat lakṣmīḥ prasīdati = as long as Lakṣmī is gracious/pleased

contentBlocks.original:
लक्ष्मि त्वयाऽलङ्कृतमानवा ये
पापैर्विमुक्ता नृपलोकमान्याः ।
गुणैर्विहीना गुणिनो भवन्ति
दुश्शीलिनः शीलवतां वरिष्ठाः ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lakṣmi tvayā'laṅkṛtamānavā yē
pāpairvimuktā nṛpalōkamānyāḥ ।
guṇairvihīnā guṇinō bhavanti
duśśīlinaḥ śīlavatāṃ variṣṭhāḥ ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 14:] O Lakṣmī — those humans adorned by you, freed from sins, become honoured among kings; even those devoid of virtues become virtuous; even those of bad character become the foremost among the virtuous.
contentBlocks.meanings: lakṣmi = O Lakṣmī | tvayā alaṅkṛtā = adorned by you | mānavāḥ = humans | yē = those who | pāpaiḥ vimuktāḥ = freed from sins | nṛpa-lōka-mānyāḥ = honoured among kings | guṇaiḥ vihīnāḥ = devoid of virtues | guṇinaḥ bhavanti = become virtuous | duḥśīlinaḥ = those of bad character | śīlavatāṃ variṣṭhāḥ = foremost among the virtuous

contentBlocks.original:
लक्ष्मीर्भूषयते रूपं लक्ष्मीर्भूषयते कुलम् ।
लक्ष्मीर्भूषयते विद्यां सर्वा लक्ष्मीर्विशिष्यते ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lakṣmīrbhūṣayatē rūpaṃ lakṣmīrbhūṣayatē kulam ।
lakṣmīrbhūṣayatē vidyāṃ sarvā lakṣmīrviśiṣyatē ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 15:] Lakṣmī adorns beauty; Lakṣmī adorns lineage; Lakṣmī adorns learning — in every way, Lakṣmī excels above all.
contentBlocks.meanings: lakṣmīḥ bhūṣayatē rūpaṃ = Lakṣmī adorns/beautifies beauty | lakṣmīḥ bhūṣayatē kulam = Lakṣmī adorns lineage | lakṣmīḥ bhūṣayatē vidyāṃ = Lakṣmī adorns learning | sarvā lakṣmīḥ viśiṣyatē = in everything, Lakṣmī excels

contentBlocks.original:
लक्ष्मी त्वद्गुणकीर्तनेन कमला भूर्यात्यलं जिह्मताम्
रुद्राद्या रविचन्द्रदेवपतयो वक्तुं च नैव क्षमाः ।
अस्माभिस्तव रूपलक्षणगुणान्वक्तुं कथं शक्यते
मातर्मां परिपाहि विश्वजननी कृत्वा ममेष्टं ध्रुवम् ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lakṣmī tvadguṇakīrtanēna kamalā bhūryātyalaṃ jihmatām
rudrādyā ravichandradēvapatayō vaktuṃ cha naiva kṣamāḥ ।
asmābhistava rūpalakṣaṇaguṇānvaktuṃ kathaṃ śakyatē
mātarmāṃ paripāhi viśvajananī kṛtvā mamēṣṭaṃ dhruvam ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 16:] O Lakṣmī — even Brahmā-Kamalā is deeply ashamed at extolling your qualities; even Rudra, the sun, the moon, and the lords of the gods are incapable of speaking them fully. How then can we speak of your form, marks, and virtues?
contentBlocks.meanings: lakṣmi = O Lakṣmī | tvad-guṇa-kīrtanēna = by the extolling of your virtues | kamalā = Brahmā/Padmā | bhūryāti alaṃ jihmatām = becomes deeply ashamed | rudrādyā = Rudra and others | ravi-chandra-dēva-patayaḥ = the lords of sun, moon, and gods | vaktuṃ = to speak | na ēva kṣamāḥ = are incapable | asmābhiḥ = by us | tava rūpa-lakṣaṇa-guṇān = your form, marks, and virtues | vaktuṃ kathaṃ śakyatē = how is it possible to speak?

contentBlocks.original:
दीनार्तिभीतं भवतापपीडितं
धनैर्विहीनं तव पार्श्वमागतम् ।
कृपानिधित्वान्मम लक्ष्मि सत्वरं
धनप्रदानाद्धननायकं कुरु ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dīnārtibhītaṃ bhavatāpapīḍitaṃ
dhanairvihīnaṃ tava pārśvamāgatam ।
kṛpānidhitvānmama lakṣmi satvaraṃ
dhanapradānāddhananāyakaṃ kuru ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 17:] Afflicted by poverty's anguish and fear, tormented by worldly suffering, devoid of wealth — yet I have come to your side. O Lakṣmī, O treasure of compassion — quickly, by granting wealth, make me the master of wealth.
contentBlocks.meanings: dīna-ārti-bhītaṃ = afflicted by the anguish and fear of the humble | bhava-tāpa-pīḍitaṃ = tormented by the burning of worldly existence | dhanaiḥ vihīnaṃ = devoid of wealth | tava pārśvam āgatam = [I] who have come to your side | kṛpā-nidhitvāt = because of your nature as a treasure of compassion | satvaraṃ = quickly | dhana-pradānāt = by the giving of wealth | dhana-nāyakaṃ kuru = make [me] the master of wealth

contentBlocks.original:
मां विलोक्य जननी हरिप्रिये
निर्धनं तव समीपमागतम् ।
देहि मे झटिति लक्ष्मि कराग्रं
वस्त्रकाञ्चनवरान्नमद्भुतम् ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
māṃ vilōkya jananī haripriyē
nirdhanaṃ tava samīpamāgatam ।
dēhi mē jhaṭiti lakṣmi karāgraṃ
vastrakāñchanavarānnamadbhutam ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 18:] O Mother, O beloved of Hari — beholding me who am penniless and have come to your presence, quickly give me, O Lakṣmī, with the tips of your hands: clothing, gold, excellent food, and wondrous things.
contentBlocks.meanings: māṃ vilōkya = beholding me | jananī = O Mother | haripriyē = O beloved of Hari | nirdhanaṃ = penniless | tava samīpam āgatam = come to your presence | dēhi mē jhaṭiti = give me quickly | lakṣmi = O Lakṣmī | karāgraṃ = with the tips of your hands | vastra = clothing | kāñchana = gold | vara-anna = excellent food | madbhutam = wondrous things

contentBlocks.original:
त्वमेव जननी लक्ष्मीः पिता लक्ष्मीस्त्वमेव च ।
भ्राता त्वं च सखा लक्ष्मीर्विद्या लक्ष्मीस्त्वमेव च ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvamēva jananī lakṣmīḥ pitā lakṣmīstvamēva cha ।
bhrātā tvaṃ cha sakhā lakṣmīrvidyā lakṣmīstvamēva cha ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 19:] You alone are my mother, Lakṣmī; you alone are my father, Lakṣmī; you are my brother and friend, Lakṣmī; you alone are my learning, Lakṣmī.
contentBlocks.meanings: tvam ēva jananī lakṣmīḥ = you alone are the mother, Lakṣmī | pitā lakṣmīḥ tvam ēva = you alone are the father, Lakṣmī | bhrātā = brother | sakhā = friend | lakṣmīḥ = Lakṣmī | vidyā lakṣmīḥ tvam ēva = you alone are the learning, Lakṣmī

contentBlocks.original:
त्राहि त्राहि महालक्ष्मि त्राहि त्राहि सुरेश्वरि ।
त्राहि त्राहि जगन्मातः दारिद्र्यात्त्राहि वेगतः ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
trāhi trāhi mahālakṣmi trāhi trāhi surēśvari ।
trāhi trāhi jaganmātaḥ dāridryāttrāhi vēgataḥ ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 20:] Save me! Save me! O Mahālakṣmī! Save me! Save me! O Queen of the gods! Save me! Save me! O Mother of the world! Swiftly save me from poverty!
contentBlocks.meanings: trāhi trāhi = save me, save me | mahālakṣmi = O Mahālakṣmī | surēśvari = O Queen of the gods | jaganmātaḥ = O Mother of the world | dāridryāt = from poverty | trāhi vēgataḥ = save quickly

contentBlocks.original:
नमस्तुभ्यं जगद्धात्रि नमस्तुभ्यं नमो नमः ।
धर्माधारे नमस्तुभ्यं नमः सम्पत्तिदायिनी ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namastubhyaṃ jagaddhātri namastubhyaṃ namō namaḥ ।
dharmādhārē namastubhyaṃ namaḥ sampattidāyinī ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 21:] Salutation to you, O upholder of the world! Salutation to you, salutation! O foundation of dharma, salutation to you! Salutation, O bestower of prosperity!
contentBlocks.meanings: jagat-dhātri = O upholder/supporter of the world | namastubhyaṃ namō namaḥ = salutation, salutation | dharma-ādhārē = O foundation of dharma | namaḥ sampatti-dāyinī = salutation, O bestower of prosperity

contentBlocks.original:
दारिद्र्यार्णवमग्नोऽहं निमग्नोऽहं रसातले ।
मज्जन्तं मां करे धृत्वा तूद्धर त्वं रमे द्रुतम् ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dāridryārṇavamagnō'haṃ nimagnō'haṃ rasātalē ।
majjantaṃ māṃ karē dhṛtvā tūddhara tvaṃ ramē drutam ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 22:] I am sunk in the ocean of poverty; I am submerged in the netherworld. O Ramē — quickly grasp me by the hand as I drown, and pull me out!
contentBlocks.meanings: dāridrya-arṇave = in the ocean of poverty | magnaḥ aham = I am sunk | nimagnō'ham = I am submerged | rasātalē = in the netherworld | majjantaṃ māṃ = me who am drowning | karē dhṛtvā = having grasped by the hand | tū uddhara = pull out | tvaṃ ramē = you, O Ramā | drutam = quickly

contentBlocks.original:
किं लक्ष्मि बहुनोक्तेन जल्पितेन पुनः पुनः ।
अन्यन्मे शरणं नास्ति सत्यं सत्यं हरिप्रिये ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kiṃ lakṣmi bahunōktēna jalpitēna punaḥ punaḥ ।
anyanmē śaraṇaṃ nāsti satyaṃ satyaṃ haripriyē ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 23:] O Lakṣmī — what is the use of saying much, of chattering on and on? Truly, truly — I have no other refuge, O beloved of Hari.
contentBlocks.meanings: kiṃ = what | lakṣmi = O Lakṣmī | bahuna uktēna = by saying much | jalpitēna punaḥ punaḥ = by chattering on and on | anyan = other | mē śaraṇaṃ nāsti = I have no refuge | satyaṃ satyaṃ = truly, truly | haripriyē = O beloved of Hari

contentBlocks.original:
एतच्छ्रुत्वाऽगस्त्यवाक्यं हृष्यमाणा हरिप्रिया ।
उवाच मधुरां वाणीं तुष्टाऽहं तव सर्वदा ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ētachChrutvā'gastyavākyaṃ hṛṣyamāṇā haripriyā ।
uvācha madhurāṃ vāṇīṃ tuṣṭā'haṃ tava sarvadā ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 24 — Lakṣmī's response:] Having heard these words of Agastya, the beloved of Hari was delighted and spoke sweet words: "I am always pleased with you."
contentBlocks.meanings: ētat śrutvā = having heard this | agastya-vākyaṃ = the words of Agastya | hṛṣyamāṇā = being delighted | hari-priyā = the beloved of Hari | uvācha = spoke | madhurāṃ vāṇīṃ = sweet words | tuṣṭā aham = I am pleased | tava sarvadā = with you always

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीलक्ष्मीरुवाच ।
यत्त्वयोक्तमिदं स्तोत्रं यः पठिष्यति मानवः ।
शृणोति च महाभागस्तस्याहं वशवर्तिनी ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīlakṣmīruvācha ।
yattvayōktamidaṃ stōtraṃ yaḥ paṭhiṣyati mānavaḥ ।
śṛṇōti cha mahābhāgastasyāhaṃ vaśavartinī ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 25 — Lakṣmī speaks:] Whatever human reads or hears this stotram that you have composed — that greatly blessed person will have me as their devoted servant.
contentBlocks.meanings: yat tvayā uktam idam stōtraṃ = this stotram spoken by you | yaḥ paṭhiṣyati mānavaḥ = whatever human will read | śṛṇōti cha = or hears | mahābhāgaḥ = the greatly blessed one | tasya ahaṃ vaśavartinī = I am their devoted servant (literally: under their control)

contentBlocks.original:
नित्यं पठति यो भक्त्या त्वलक्ष्मीस्तस्य नश्यति ।
ऋणं च नश्यते तीव्रं वियोगं नैव पश्यति ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nityaṃ paṭhati yō bhaktyā tvalakṣmīstasya naśyati ।
ṛṇaṃ cha naśyatē tīvraṃ viyōgaṃ naiva paśyati ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Phalaśruti verse 1:] Whoever reads this daily with devotion — their misfortune (alakṣmī) will be destroyed; their severe debt will be eliminated; they will experience no separation [from loved ones].
contentBlocks.meanings: nityaṃ paṭhati = reads daily | yaḥ bhaktyā = whoever with devotion | tvat-alakṣmīḥ = their alakṣmī (misfortune) | tasya naśyati = will be destroyed | ṛṇaṃ = debt | naśyatē tīvraṃ = severe [debt] will be eliminated | viyōgaṃ naiva paśyati = will not experience separation

contentBlocks.original:
यः पठेत्प्रातरुत्थाय श्रद्धाभक्तिसमन्वितः ।
गृहे तस्य सदा तिष्टेन्नित्यं श्रीः पतिना सह ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yaḥ paṭhētprātarutthāya śraddhābhaktisamanvitaḥ ।
gṛhē tasya sadā tiṣṭēnnityaṃ śrīḥ patinā saha ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Phalaśruti verse 2:] Whoever reads this, rising early in the morning with faith and devotion — in their home, Śrī will permanently dwell with her Lord.
contentBlocks.meanings: yaḥ paṭhēt prātar utthāya = whoever reads having risen early in the morning | śraddhā-bhakti-samanvitaḥ = endowed with faith and devotion | gṛhē tasya sadā tiṣṭhēt = in their home will always dwell | nityaṃ śrīḥ patinā saha = Śrī permanently with her Lord

contentBlocks.original:
सुखसौभाग्यसम्पन्नो मनस्वी बुद्धिमान्भवेत् ।
पुत्रवान् गुणवान् श्रेष्ठो भोगभोक्ता च मानवः ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sukhasaubhāgyasampannō manasvī buddhimānbhavēt ।
putravān guṇavān śrēṣṭhō bhōgabhōktā cha mānavaḥ ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Phalaśruti verse 3:] That person will be endowed with happiness and good fortune; of noble mind and good intellect; will have sons, be virtuous, excellent, and an enjoyer of life's pleasures.
contentBlocks.meanings: sukha-saubhāgya-sampannaḥ = endowed with happiness and good fortune | manasvī = noble-minded | buddhimān = of good intellect | bhavēt = will be | putra-vān = having sons | guṇa-vān = virtuous | śrēṣṭhaḥ = excellent | bhōga-bhōktā = enjoyer of pleasures | cha mānavaḥ = and that person

contentBlocks.original:
इदं स्तोत्रं महापुण्यं लक्ष्म्यागस्त्यप्रकीर्तितम् ।
विष्णुप्रसादजननं चतुर्वर्गफलप्रदम् ॥ 29 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
idaṃ stōtraṃ mahāpuṇyaṃ lakṣmyāgastyaprakīrtitam ।
viṣṇuprasādajananaṃ chaturvargaphalapradam ॥ 29 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Phalaśruti verse 4:] This highly meritorious stotram, proclaimed by Lakṣmī and Agastya, generates the grace of Viṣṇu and bestows the fruits of all four aims of life.
contentBlocks.meanings: idam stōtram = this stotram | mahā-puṇyam = highly meritorious | lakṣmyā agastya-prakīrtitam = proclaimed by Lakṣmī and Agastya | viṣṇu-prasāda-jananam = generating the grace of Viṣṇu | chatur-varga-phala-pradam = bestowing the fruits of the four aims of life (dharma, artha, kāma, mōkṣa)

contentBlocks.original:
राजद्वारे जयश्चैव शत्रोश्चैव पराजयः ।
भूतप्रेतपिशाचानां व्याघ्राणां न भयं तथा ॥ 30 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rājadvārē jayaśchaiva śatrōśchaiva parājayaḥ ।
bhūtaprētapiśāchānāṃ vyāghrāṇāṃ na bhayaṃ tathā ॥ 30 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Phalaśruti verse 5:] [Through its power:] Victory at the court of kings; defeat of enemies; no fear from ghosts, pretas, demons, or tigers.
contentBlocks.meanings: rāja-dvārē = at the king's court/gate | jayaḥ cha ēva = victory indeed | śatrōḥ cha ēva parājayaḥ = defeat of the enemy too | bhūta-prēta-piśāchānāṃ = from ghosts, pretas, and demons | vyāghrāṇāṃ = from tigers | na bhayaṃ = no fear | tathā = likewise

contentBlocks.original:
न शस्त्रानलतोयौघाद्भयं तस्य प्रजायते ।
दुर्वृत्तानां च पापानां बहुहानिकरं परम् ॥ 31 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
na śastrānalatōyaughādbhayaṃ tasya prajāyatē ।
durvṛttānāṃ cha pāpānāṃ bahuhānikaraṃ param ॥ 31 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Phalaśruti verse 6:] No fear will arise for them from weapons, fire, or floods. It causes great harm to the wicked and sinful.
contentBlocks.meanings: na śastrāt = not from weapons | anala = fire | tōya-aughāt = from floods | bhayaṃ = fear | tasya prajāyatē = arises for them | durvṛttānāṃ = for the wicked | cha pāpānāṃ = and the sinful | bahu-hānikaraṃ = causing great harm | param = supremely/highly

contentBlocks.original:
मन्दुराकरिशालासु गवां गोष्ठे समाहितः ।
पठेत्तद्दोषशान्त्यर्थं महापातकनाशनम् ॥ 32 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mandurākariśālāsu gavāṃ gōṣṭhē samāhitaḥ ।
paṭhēttaddōṣaśāntyarthaṃ mahāpātakanāśanam ॥ 32 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Phalaśruti verse 7:] Whoever, with collected mind, reads this for the pacification of faults — even in stables, elephant-halls, and cowsheds — it destroys all great sins.
contentBlocks.meanings: mandurā = horse-stable | kari-śālāsu = in elephant-halls | gavāṃ gōṣṭhē = in cowsheds | samāhitaḥ = with collected/concentrated mind | paṭhēt = reads | tad-dōṣa-śānti-arthaṃ = for the pacification of those faults | mahā-pātaka-nāśanam = destroys great sins

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वसौख्यकरं नॄणामायुरारोग्यदं तथा ।
अगस्त्यमुनिना प्रोक्तं प्रजानां हितकाम्यया ॥ 33 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvasaukhyakaraṃ nṝṇāmāyurārōgyadaṃ tathā ।
agastyamuninā prōktaṃ prajānāṃ hitakāmyayā ॥ 33 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Phalaśruti verse 8:] Creator of all happiness for humanity, bestower of long life and health — this [stotram] was proclaimed by the sage Agastya out of his compassionate desire for the welfare of all people.
contentBlocks.meanings: sarva-saukhya-karaṃ = creator of all happiness | nṝṇām = for humanity | āyur-ārogya-daṃ = bestower of life and health | tathā = likewise | agastya-muninā = by the sage Agastya | prōktaṃ = proclaimed | prajānāṃ = for the people | hita-kāmyayā = out of desire for [their] welfare
